title: Cerastium julicum
taxonomy: Cerastium julicum is classified within the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the carnation or pink family. This family consists of flowering plants that are primarily found in temperate and subtropical regions. Cerastium julicum is further classified into the genus Cerastium, which comprises approximately 60 species of flowering plants. The genus Cerastium belongs to the order Caryophyllales, a group of flowering plants that are primarily characterized by their five-petaled flowers. intro: Cerastium julicum, also known as Snow-in-Summer, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Cerastium julicum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Herbal preparations made from Cerastium julicum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote relaxation.
